latest.title: Alludium Further Investment, Warrants & Update
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 07:30
RNS Number : 5676W
Catenai PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Catenai PLC
("Catenai", the "Company")
Alludium
Further Investment
Company Update
Issue of Warrants
Catenai PLC (AIM: CTAI), the AIM-quoted provider of technology and digital solutions, is pleased to announce that it has made a further investment of £250,000 (the "Investment") into Alludium Ltd ("Alludium), a developer of a no-code AI Agent Operating System.
The Investment is part of a £1 million fundraising by Alludium at a pre-money valuation of £9 million, which will increase Catenai's shareholding from approximately 13% to 16.1%. The Investment follows Alludium's successful public-commercial launch, announced on 10 March 2026, with the funds to be used for marketing initiatives and to further enhance the Alludium platform.
Alludium is a pre-revenue company and its first full year results have been published and report a loss of approximately £33,000 for the year ended 31 August 2024.
The Investment will be made from existing cash reserves and Catenai confirms that it continues to have sufficient cash resources to cover corporate costs beyond the next 18 months from the date of this announcement.
Company Update
Furthermore, Klarian Limited ("Klarian"), a company to which Catenai provided a £450,000 unsecured convertible loan note facility, will provide an update in early April 2026 and will be followed by a presentation via the Investor Meet Company platform. As announced on 5 January 2026, Klarian is due to repay Catenai
the £624,250 under the convertible loan note agreement announced on 25 April 2024, and related fees by 31 March 2026, with an additional fee of £74,910 as part of an arrangement if the repayment was to occur after 28 February 2026.
The Company is also pleased to advise that its client, Charlton Athletic Community Trust, has engaged the Company to undertake a new technology services project. The Company will receive a modest cash fee for the project.
Separately, the Board is reviewing the Company's Bitcoin treasury policy, having not yet made any purchases, and will update the market in due course.
Issue of Warrants
Following the announcement of 10 March 2025, the Company has issued the 100,000,000 warrants over new ordinary shares in the Company to the founders of Alludium which are exercisable at 0.3p and may be exercised for a period of 18 months from the date of this announcement.
Investor presentation via Investor Meet Company
The Company is pleased to announce that John Farthing, John Frizelle and Barry Downes will provide a live presentation relating to Alludium via Investor Meet Company on 19 March 2026, 09:00 GMT.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 18 March 2026, 09:00 GMT, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet CATENAI PLC via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/catenai-plc/register-investor
Investors who already follow CATENAI PLC on the Investor Meet Company platform will automatically be invited.
John Farthing, Interim CEO of Catenai Plc, commented:
"We are delighted to have made our second investment in Alludium following its successful public launch. We believe the platform has the capability to revolutionise the business landscape through automation, boosting corporate growth and employee productivity. Catenai will continue to be in a strong financial position following the investment and looks forward to providing the market with further updates."
Barry Downes, Chairman of Alludium, commented:
"Catenai has been a hugely supportive shareholder, so we are thrilled to have them participate in our latest fundraising. Following Alludium's public launch, the new funds will be used to further expand the platform's capabilities as well as marketing and commercial development."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. The person who arranged for release of this announcement on behalf of the Company was John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer of the Company and the Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.
For further information please contact:
Catenai PLC
+44 (0)20 7183 8666
John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer
Cairn Financial Advisers LLP (Nominated Adviser)
+44(0)20 7213 0880

About Catenai PLC
Catenai is an AIM quoted provider of digital media and technology services. The Company has an experienced IT team of project managers and integrators who have deployed systems across corporate, government and educational sectors.
www.catenaiplc.com

latest.title: Update re Alludium
latest.content:
19 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5705T
Catenai PLC
19 February 2026
CATENAI PLC
("Catenai" or the "Company")
Alludium Recognised as Select Tier Partner in Google Cloud Partner Network
Catenai PLC (AIM: CTAI), the AIM quoted provider of technology and digital solutions, notes that Alludium, its investee company and developer of a no-code AI Agent Operating System, has been recognised as a Select tier partner within the Google Cloud Partner Network.
Select tier status is an earned designation within Google Cloud's updated partner model, recognising demonstrated proficiency and customer impact across Google Cloud and Google Workspace. The recognition reflects Alludium's technical alignment and integration capabilities across key Google Workspace tools, including Gmail, Google Calendar and Google Drive.
Alludium's platform enables organisations to design and deploy collaborative AI agents that operate across real-world business systems. Through its Agent Operating System ("Agent OS"), Alludium aims to move AI adoption beyond conversational assistance towards practical task execution and workflow automation.
The Directors believe that this recognition further validates Alludium's commercial positioning and supports its strategy of building enterprise-grade integrations within established cloud ecosystems.
The person who arranged for the release of this announcement on behalf of the Company was John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer of the Company, and the Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.
John Frizelle, Founder & CEO of Alludium, commented:
"Select Tier recognition is an important milestone for Alludium. It reinforces what we are building: an Agent OS that helps teams delegate real work, not just chat about it, through practical and integrated workflows. To make that possible, we need to properly integrate with the infrastructure organisations already rely on and partner with leading providers such as Google Cloud to ensure secure and scalable adoption."
John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer of Catenai Plc, commented
:
"We are pleased to see Alludium recognised within the Google Cloud Partner Network at the Select tier. This milestone reflects the progress the team has made in building an enterprise-aligned Agent Operating System capable of operating across widely adopted business tools. It is encouraging validation of Alludium's commercial positioning, and we look forward to supporting its continued development and market expansion."
Further information on the announcement can be found on the
Alludium blog
.
About Catenai PLC
Catenai is an AIM-quoted technology company focused on delivering digital solutions that address commercial challenges and support long-term value creation. The Company specialises in secure, app-based platforms and has an increasing focus on artificial intelligence and emerging technologies, including its investment in Alludium, which is developing a no-code AI agent workforce platform.
http://www.catenaiplc.com
This communication is a "Reach" announcement. Reach is a non-regulatory news service. By using this service an issuer is confirming that the information contained in this announcement is of a non-regulatory nature. Information required to be notified under the AIM Rules for Companies, Market Abuse Regulation or other regulation would be disseminated as an RNS regulatory announcement and not on RNS Reach.

latest.title: Klarian update
latest.content:
5 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5412N
Catenai PLC
05 January 2026
5 January 2026
Catenai PLC
("Catenai", the "Company")
Klarian update
Catenai PLC (AIM: CTAI), the AIM quoted provider of digital media and technology services, issues an update on Klarian Ltd ("Klarian"), a company to which Catenai provided a £450,000 unsecured convertible loan note facility ("CLN") as per the announcement of 25 April 2024.
Further to the announcements on 7 April 2025 and 30 June 2025, the Company has entered into a further extension agreement ("Extension Agreement") with Klarian. Under the terms of the Extension Agreement, Klarian will repay Catenai the £624,250 due under the CLN and related fees by 31 March 2026 (the "Repayment"). If Repayment occurs after 28 February 2026, Catenai will be due an additional fee of £74,910 ("Extension Fee"). If Repayment occurs prior to 28 February 2026, then Catenai will receive a proportion of the Extension Fee.
In addition, the Company intends to hold an investor webinar with Rob Clegg, CEO of Klarian, via the Investor Meet Company platform. The presentation will provide an opportunity for Klarian to introduce its business to Catenai's shareholders and summarise its positive progress through 2025. A further announcement confirming the exact date and time for the webinar will be made in due course.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. The person who arranged for release of this announcement on behalf of the Company was John Farthing, Interim Chief Executive Officer of the Company and the Directors of the Company are responsible for the release of this announcement.

company_30s.what_they_do: Catenai plc builds AI‑powered software that helps businesses automate data analysis, customer interaction and decision‑making. Their flagship product, Alludium, is a cloud‑based platform that turns raw data into actionable insights for enterprises.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The AI market is expanding faster than any other tech sector, with enterprises spending billions on analytics and automation. Catenai’s Alludium platform offers a unique blend of machine‑learning models and a user‑friendly interface, giving it a competitive edge in a crowded space.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a growth phase, raising capital through warrants and a recent loan repayment. It has recently launched a new website and announced several investor presentations, signalling a push to secure more funding and showcase product traction. The shares trade at a very low price, reflecting limited liquidity and a heavy reliance on future funding rounds.

rns_narrative: Catenai is busy proving the value of Alludium. Recent filings show a series of investor presentations, a fresh website launch, and a new round of warrants to raise capital. The company is also updating its holdings and funding strategy, indicating it is actively managing its balance sheet.

The narrative clusters around three themes: 1) Alludium’s development and investment updates, 2) Capital‑raising and equity structuring, and 3) Operational milestones such as website launch and product demos. Each theme signals a move toward establishing credibility with investors and customers.

These events suggest the company is stabilising its operations and preparing for a public market reaction. The repeated focus on Alludium indicates a belief that the product will be the main catalyst for future growth.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – traders are waiting for the next Alludium presentation to see if the platform will finally deliver.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of posts predict a sharp rise after the 19‑Jan investor presentation, with one trader even calling it a “big spike into the .50s.”
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some skeptics point to the company’s cash burn and the fact that Alludium has yet to generate revenue, warning that the stock could stay stagnant or decline further if funding stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from neutral to mildly bullish as the 19‑Jan presentation approaches, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning there is no public buzz. This silence gives a contrarian edge – a surprise news event could trigger a big move.

near_term_catalysts: The investor presentation scheduled for 19‑Jan‑2026 – a positive Q&A or a new funding announcement could spark a breakout.
The 13‑Mar‑2026 Alludium investment update – any sign of new customers or revenue will validate the platform.
A volume spike that pushes the price above the 0.33p resistance level mentioned in recent posts, signalling a potential shift from capitulation to accumulation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for: 1) A clear statement of product traction or new customer wins, 2) a share volume jump of at least 2‑3 times the average, and 3) a price move past 0.33p with sustained buying pressure.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a recovery. The APEX score of 11/21 shows moderate compression, suggesting the next move could be a short‑term breakout once the presentation material is released.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the Alludium platform gains traction and the investor presentation delivers positive news, the price could climb from its current low to 0.33p or higher. That would represent a 200‑300% upside from today’s level, and the risk is limited to the current share price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still lacks revenue and has a high cash burn. If it fails to secure additional funding or Alludium doesn’t gain traction, the shares could stay near zero or decline further, especially if new dilution occurs.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 100% of the invested capital (the share could stay flat or fall to zero). Potential gain: 200‑300% upside if the price reaches 0.33p or beyond. The asymmetry favors the upside while keeping downside capped at the current price.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed breakout above 0.33p with volume at least twice the average daily volume. Size the position to 5% of your portfolio and set a stop just below 0.25p to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the 19‑Jan presentation or the 13‑Mar Alludium update. Watch for a clear statement of product adoption or new funding; if either occurs, consider a quick entry on a breakout.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks, so the only real risk is the stock staying stagnant.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CTAI.L sits on the brink of a potentially explosive rebound once the Alludium investment update surfaces, offering huge upside with a limited downside if the company can prove its AI platform.
